meta.title: 4 Days Bali Gates of Heaven — Lempuyang Temple Tour from RM1,088 | Dhesu Travel
meta.description: Photograph the iconic Gates of Heaven at Lempuyang Temple on this 4-day private Bali tour from RM1,088. Includes Uluwatu, Kecak Fire Dance, Jimbaran Bay seafood, Ubud highlands, Mount Batur, Tirta Empul, Tirta Gangga water palace and Kanto Lampo Waterfall. Daily departures.
meta.keywords: Bali Gates of Heaven, Lempuyang Temple tour, 4 days Bali, Tirta Gangga water palace, Kanto Lampo Waterfall, Uluwatu temple, Ubud highlands, Mount Batur, Tirta Empul, Bali photography tour, Bali package Malaysia RM1088

hero.title: 4 Days Bali
hero.intro: Stand before one of the world's most iconic photographs — the Heaven's Gate split portal at Lempuyang Temple with Mount Agung framed between — on this extraordinary private Bali journey combining ancient temples, volcanic highlands and hidden waterfalls.
hero.heroImageAlt: The Gates of Heaven split portal at Lempuyang Temple with Mount Agung in the background, East Bali

quickFacts.saving: Save RM272 per person (20% online discount)
includes: Meals as specified (3 breakfasts, 3 lunches, 3 dinners)
excludes: ETIQA Travel Insurance Area 2 Gold RM41
Optional Alas Arum Bali Swing RM68

overview.heading: Ancient Temples, Volcanic Highlands and Bali's Most Iconic Photography Spot
body: The 'Gates of Heaven' is one of the most iconic images in all of travel photography — the ancient split portal of Pura Lempuyang Luhur, with the symmetrical silhouette of Mount Agung perfectly framed between its carved stone walls, reflected in a still pool of water. Visiting this extraordinary site is a bucket-list moment for any traveller to Bali, and this 4-day private package is built around that extraordinary experience, combining it with the very best of Bali's cultural, natural and spiritual highlights.

Over four days, you will explore the clifftop drama of Uluwatu, dine on the sands of Jimbaran Bay, journey into the cool Ubud highlands for art villages and volcano panoramas, visit one of Bali's most sacred holy spring temples, and on Day 3 travel east to discover the Gates of Heaven, the enchanting Tirta Gangga royal water palace and the lush cascade of Kanto Lampo Waterfall. This is Bali seen deeply — not just its most famous images, but the stories, history and beauty that make each site profoundly meaningful.

items.title: Lempuyang Temple — The Gates of Heaven
items.body: Perched on the slopes of Mount Lempuyang in East Bali, this ancient temple complex — one of Bali's nine directional temples — is home to the world-famous Candi Bentar split gateway known as the 'Gates of Heaven'. When reflected in the mirror-still pool before the gate, with the perfect cone of Mount Agung completing the composition behind, it creates one of the most extraordinary photographic moments in all of travel. Arrive early with your private guide for the best light and shortest queues.
items.imageAlt: The iconic Gates of Heaven split portal at Lempuyang Temple with Mount Agung reflected in the foreground pool, East Bali

items.title: Tirta Gangga Royal Water Palace
items.body: Built in 1948 by the last Raja of Karangasem, Tirta Gangga is one of Bali's most beautiful and serene royal monuments — a complex of tiered stone fountains, ornamental fish ponds, mossy statues, stepping stone paths and lush gardens fed by sacred spring water. Set against a backdrop of terraced rice fields with Mount Agung in the distance, it is a place of extraordinary peace and beauty.
items.imageAlt: Tirta Gangga royal water palace with ornamental fountains and stepping stones, East Bali

items.title: Kanto Lampo Waterfall
items.body: Hidden in a lush tropical ravine just outside Ubud, Kanto Lampo Waterfall is one of Bali's most photogenic natural attractions — a wide cascade of water tumbling over naturally stepped volcanic rock into a crystal pool below. At the right angle and in good light, the waterfall creates a magical curtain of misty white water surrounded by dense jungle green.
items.imageAlt: Kanto Lampo Waterfall cascading over stepped volcanic rock into a jungle pool near Ubud, Bali

items.title: Uluwatu Cliff Temple & Optional Kecak Fire Dance
items.body: Open your Bali journey on the edge of a 70-metre sea cliff at the sacred Uluwatu Temple, one of Bali's most dramatically positioned spiritual sites. The clifftop walk with its sweeping Indian Ocean panorama is extraordinary at any time of day, but the optional sunset Kecak Fire Dance performance (RM35, self-paid) adds an unforgettable dimension of traditional Balinese culture.
items.imageAlt: Uluwatu Temple on the edge of a high sea cliff above the Indian Ocean at sunset, Bali

items.title: Mount Batur Volcano Panorama Lunch
items.body: Enjoy a spectacular Indonesian buffet lunch on the rim of the ancient Batur caldera at Grand Puncak Sari restaurant, with an unobstructed panoramic view of Mount Batur's active volcanic cone and the vast blue Lake Batur below — one of the most dramatic dining settings in Southeast Asia.
items.imageAlt: Panoramic view of Mount Batur active volcano and Lake Batur from Kintamani highlands, Bali

items.title: Tirta Empul Sacred Holy Spring Temple
items.body: One of Bali's most sacred and atmospheric temple complexes, Tirta Empul is built around a holy spring where the waters have been used for Balinese Hindu purification rituals for over a thousand years. The devotion, the beautiful temple architecture, the sound of flowing sacred water and the fragrance of frangipani offerings make this a profoundly moving experience.
items.imageAlt: Sacred Tirta Empul holy spring temple with purification pools and Balinese devotees, Ubud

itinerary.heading: Your 4-Day Bali Gates of Heaven Journey

days.title: Melasti Beach, Uluwatu & Jimbaran Bay Seafood Dinner
days.description: Your Bali adventure begins with a warm airport greeting and transfer to your hotel. Once settled, head to the breathtaking Melasti Beach — one of Bali's most scenically dramatic coastal escapes, tucked behind towering limestone cliffs with pristine white sand and vivid turquoise water. As the afternoon deepens into golden hour, travel to the clifftop Uluwatu Temple, one of Bali's six sacred Kayangan Jagat directional temples, perched 70 metres above the crashing Indian Ocean on the island's southwestern tip. The clifftop walk through the temple complex — with vast ocean views in every direction and mischievous monkeys as companions — is a truly memorable experience. An optional Kecak Fire Dance performance at sunset (RM35, self-paid) adds an extraordinary cultural dimension before you descend to Jimbaran Bay for the quintessential Bali dinner — fresh grilled BBQ seafood served beachside as the stars emerge over the ocean.
suggestedActivities: Melasti Beach — dramatic limestone cliff cove
Uluwatu Temple — clifftop sea temple walk
Optional: Kecak Fire Dance at sunset (RM35, self-paid)
Grilled BBQ seafood dinner at Jimbaran Bay

days.title: Ubud Cultural Heart, Mount Batur Volcano Views & Tirta Empul Temple
days.description: After a satisfying hotel breakfast, your private guide takes you up into the cool and culturally rich Ubud highlands for a full day of discovery. Begin in Celuk Village, where master goldsmiths and silversmiths have practised their craft for generations — the perfect place to find an authentic Balinese jewellery piece at workshop prices. Next, explore the magnificent Ubud Royal Palace (Puri Saren Agung) with its elaborate carvings and traditional Balinese courtyard design, then browse the colourful Ubud Traditional Market for silk scarves, wooden statues, handmade kites and local souvenirs. Visit the famous Tegalalang Rice Terrace — one of Asia's most photographed landscapes — and opt for the Alas Arum Bali Swing (RM68) for an aerial view above the jungle valley. A stop at a local coffee plantation allows tastings of Luwak coffee and herbal teas before the spectacular Indonesian buffet lunch at Grand Puncak Sari on the rim of the Kintamani caldera, with sweeping views of active Mount Batur and Lake Batur. The day's spiritual centrepiece is Tirta Empul Temple, where sacred spring waters have flowed for over a millennium and Balinese devotees perform purification rituals believed to restore health and spiritual balance. Dinner at Fork Bakery and Grill Kuta rounds off an exceptional day.
suggestedActivities: Celuk Gold & Silver Village
Ubud Royal Palace (Puri Saren Agung)
Ubud Traditional Market
Tegalalang Rice Terrace
Optional: Alas Arum Bali Swing (RM68, self-paid)
Local coffee plantation — Luwak coffee tasting
Kintamani volcano panorama lunch at Grand Puncak Sari
Tirta Empul Sacred Holy Spring Temple
Dinner at Fork Bakery and Grill Kuta

days.title: Gates of Heaven, Tirta Gangga Water Palace & Kanto Lampo Waterfall
days.description: The third day is the most extraordinary of your journey — a trip east through Bali's rural heartland to some of its most sacred and beautiful sites. Depart early for Pura Lempuyang Luhur, one of Bali's nine directional temples and home to the legendary 'Gates of Heaven'. The ancient Candi Bentar split gateway, perfectly framing the majestic cone of Mount Agung in the distance — and reflected in the mirror-still ornamental pool before it — is one of the most iconic and most photographed images in all of travel. Your guide will ensure you get the best timing and composition for your photographs. After exploring the temple grounds with their panoramic mountain views, head to lunch at Maha Gangga restaurant, beautifully set in a rice field with mountain backdrop. The afternoon brings two contrasting gems: the Tirta Gangga Royal Water Palace — a serene complex of tiered ornamental fountains, fish ponds, mossy stone sculptures and tropical gardens built by the last King of Karangasem — and the Kanto Lampo Waterfall, a stunning wide cascade over stepped volcanic rock in a lush jungle ravine. Dinner of the famous spiced Ayam Betutu traditional Balinese chicken completes a truly unforgettable day.
suggestedActivities: Pura Lempuyang Luhur — Gates of Heaven photography
Temple complex exploration with Mount Agung panoramas
Lunch at Maha Gangga restaurant in rice field setting
Tirta Gangga Royal Water Palace and gardens
Kanto Lampo Waterfall — jungle cascade
Dinner featuring Ayam Betutu traditional Balinese chicken

days.title: Free Morning & Departure
days.description: Your final morning in Bali is completely free — perfect for a beach walk, a traditional Balinese massage, a visit to a local café for one last acai bowl and fresh coconut, or simply sitting by the hotel pool watching Bali ease into another tropical day. At the appointed time, check out and visit a quality souvenir shop for last-minute gifts — Balinese coffee blends, batik fabric, silver jewellery and artisan crafts — before your comfortable driver transfer to Ngurah Rai International Airport.
suggestedActivities: Free morning — beach, spa, café or pool
Hotel checkout
Souvenir shop visit for gifts and local products
Airport transfer for departure

whyBook.heading: Why Book Your Bali Gates of Heaven Tour Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: Dhesu Travel has been organising Bali tours for Malaysian travellers since 1988. The Gates of Heaven at Lempuyang Temple is one of the most in-demand experiences on the island — we know how to time the visit for the best light, shortest queues and the most extraordinary photographs.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed with MATTA and IATA, Dhesu provides complete professional reliability and consumer protection for every booking.

points.title: Trusted Since 1988
points.body: Over 35 years of deep knowledge about Bali — including the best timing, routes and tactics for visiting the Gates of Heaven to avoid the crowds.

points.title: 100% Private Tours
points.body: Your private vehicle and dedicated guide means you can reach Lempuyang Temple early for the best light and the most peaceful experience — no waiting for a group coach.

points.title: Photography-Optimised Itinerary
points.body: Our East Bali day is carefully timed so you visit the Gates of Heaven in the best morning light, with Tirta Gangga and Kanto Lampo in the cooler afternoon — maximising both comfort and photographic opportunity.

sections.heading: Lempuyang Temple — The Story Behind the Gates of Heaven
sections.body: Pura Lempuyang Luhur is one of Bali's nine Kahyangan Jagat (directional temples) and one of the island's oldest and most sacred sites, believed to date back more than a thousand years. It sits at the base of the 1,175-metre Mount Lempuyang in the Karangasem Regency of East Bali, and the journey to the temple gates through a forested hill corridor adds to the sense of sacred pilgrimage. The famous 'Gates of Heaven' — the split Candi Bentar gateway — is just the first of seven temple gates at the site, each one ascending higher up the mountain. It is the first gate and its reflection pool that create the iconic image seen around the world.

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit the Gates of Heaven
sections.body: The Gates of Heaven photograph is best captured in the early morning when the light is soft and golden and the queue for the reflection pool is shorter. Our private tour departs from your hotel early on Day 3 to ensure you arrive at Lempuyang in the best conditions. The journey from Kuta to Lempuyang takes approximately 2–2.5 hours, so an early start is essential for the iconic experience. Clear skies are most reliable during Bali's dry season (April–October), when Mount Agung is more likely to be visible behind the gates.

sections.heading: East Bali — Bali's Hidden Treasure
sections.body: Most Bali packages focus on the south and centre of the island — Kuta, Seminyak, Ubud and the Bedugul highlands. But East Bali, home to Lempuyang Temple, Tirta Gangga and the unspoiled fishing villages of the Karangasem coast, represents a side of the island that fewer tourists reach. The landscape here is more dramatic — dominated by the towering presence of Gunung Agung, Bali's sacred volcano and highest peak — and the atmosphere is quieter and more authentically rural than the tourist-heavy south. Adding East Bali to your itinerary gives you a genuinely different dimension of the island.

items.question: Is the famous water reflection at the Gates of Heaven real?
items.answer: The reflection you see in many photographs is created using a small mirror or bowl of water held below the camera lens — it is a photography technique used at the site rather than a naturally occurring reflection pool. Your guide will assist you in capturing the best version of this iconic shot. Genuine reflections are sometimes visible after rain when water collects on the stone platform.

items.question: How early do we need to arrive at Lempuyang Temple?
items.answer: We recommend arriving as early as possible — ideally before 8am — for the shortest queues at the reflection pool and the best morning light. Our itinerary is designed to depart your hotel early on Day 3 to facilitate this. The queue for the iconic photograph can extend to 1–2 hours during peak season if you arrive later.

items.question: What should I wear to Lempuyang Temple?
items.answer: As with all Balinese Hindu temples, visitors must wear a sarong and sash to enter the temple grounds. These are provided for rent at the site for a small fee. We recommend wearing comfortable walking shoes as the temple path involves steps and uneven ground.

items.question: What meals are included?
items.answer: Day 1: Dinner (Jimbaran Bay BBQ seafood). Day 2: Breakfast, lunch (Kintamani volcano viewpoint), dinner (Fork Bakery and Grill). Day 3: Breakfast, lunch (Maha Gangga restaurant in rice field), dinner (Ayam Betutu traditional chicken). Day 4: Breakfast. Total: 3 breakfasts, 2 lunches, 3 dinners.

items.question: How far is Lempuyang Temple from Kuta?
items.answer: Lempuyang Temple (Pura Lempuyang Luhur) is located in the Karangasem Regency in East Bali, approximately 80km from Kuta — roughly 2–2.5 hours by private vehicle depending on traffic. The drive is itself beautiful, passing through rice fields, traditional villages and the slopes of Mount Agung.

items.question: Can I add Nusa Penida to this itinerary?
items.answer: Yes — Nusa Penida can be added as an extra day to this package, making it a 5-day tour. We would restructure the itinerary slightly to accommodate the fast boat to Nusa Penida. Contact our team to discuss the best way to combine these two extraordinary experiences.
